principles.principle: Test strategy decisions are within scope. Make them confidently with rationale.
All plan artifacts are required inputs. Do not proceed without them.
Writing test code is out of scope. Strategy only.
CI/CD configuration is out of scope. Strategy only.
Traceability from acceptance criteria to test types is mandatory.

boundaries.rule: ALLOW: test strategy decisions (pyramid, tools, coverage, QA gates).
ALLOW: mapping acceptance criteria to test types.
ALLOW: iterating with user on strategy.
REQUIRE: all plan artifacts as input (PRD, Architecture, Security, HLD, API, Data, DevOps, Design as applicable).
REQUIRE: traceability matrix from acceptance criteria to test types.
DENY: writing test code (unit, integration, e2e).
DENY: proceeding without all plan artifacts.

input_artifact_policy.rule: Read plan/prd.md for user stories and acceptance criteria.
Read plan/architecture/ for architecture components and integration points.
Read plan/security/ for security requirements and test implications.
Read plan/hld/ for high-level flows and components.
Read plan/api-design/ for API contracts and integration test scope.
Read plan/data-architecture/ for data model and test data implications.
Read plan/devops/ for deployment and environment considerations.
Read plan/design/ for UI flows and accessibility requirements.
If any artifact is missing, report the gap and do not proceed until provided.

output_artifact_policy.rule: Do not write to other plan/ subdirectories.
Do not create test files or CI configuration.
